title: Work-Life Balance Is a Process, Not an Achievement
subtitle: 工作与生活的平衡是过程而非终点

paragraphs.en: Few topics have been so endlessly analysed as work-life balance. The quest to attain this mysterious state has dominated discussion around careers for years — especially for working parents. The concept is often presented as something to achieve, or a goal to reach. And once you've reached it, congratulations: you've made it; you're a successful human being of the 21st century.
paragraphs.zh: 很少有话题像工作与生活的平衡这样被没完没了地分析。多年来，对这种神秘状态的追求一直主导着关于职业生涯的讨论——对职场父母而言尤其如此。这一概念常常被描绘成某种需要实现的成果，或是一个需要达成的目标。而一旦你达成了，恭喜你：你做到了；你是一个成功的二十一世纪人类了。

paragraphs.en: But the problem is that we often tell ourselves: "I'm going to put in eight hours' worth of work, and then I'm going to put in eight hours' worth of 'me time', which will include my family, my hobbies, my workout, my everything," says Anat Lechner, clinical associate professor of management at New York University. "I don't think it's such a simple formula."
paragraphs.zh: 但问题在于，我们常常告诉自己："我要投入八小时工作，然后再投入八小时'自我时间'，这其中要包括家庭、爱好、健身，以及我的一切，"纽约大学管理学临床副教授阿娜特·莱希纳说。"我不认为这是什么简单的公式。"

paragraphs.en: And, according to new findings, it may not be. Some researchers are now encouraging us to stop thinking about work-life balance as an achievement that you either hit or don't. Instead, they suggest it may be more of a lifelong process — a continuous, never-ending exercise that requires self-awareness and timely adjustments. Researchers Ioana Lupu and Mayra Ruiz-Castro argue that work-life balance is "a cycle, not an achievement." In their 2020 study, the researchers interviewed nearly 80 employees at two London-based firms — an equal number of men and women between the ages of 30 and 50, all with at least one dependent child — who worked in middle or senior management roles.
paragraphs.zh: 而根据最新的研究发现，事实可能的确如此。如今一些研究人员鼓励我们不要再将工作与生活的平衡视为一个要么达成、要么失败的成就。相反，他们认为这更像是一个终生的过程——一项持续不断、永无止境的练习，需要自我觉察和适时调整。研究人员约安娜·卢普和迈拉·鲁伊斯-卡斯特罗认为，工作与生活的平衡是"一个循环，而非一项成就"。在2020年的研究中，他们采访了伦敦两家公司的近80名员工——男女数量相等，年龄在30至50岁之间，都至少有一个受抚养的子女，且均担任中高级管理职务。

words.meaning: n. 自我意识；自知之明

paragraphs.en: Although it sounds like the respondents had a lot in common, here's what separated them: about 30% of the men and 50% of the women reported resisting working long hours. The other respondents, meanwhile, all worked long hours because they thought that's what successful professionals should do.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管受访者听起来有许多共同点，但区别在于：大约30%的男性和50%的女性表示自己会抵制长时间工作。与此同时，其余受访者则全部长时间工作，因为他们认为这是成功专业人士应有的样子。

paragraphs.en: Lupu and Ruiz-Castro looked at those who rejected the long hours and they found that those workers actually had strikingly similar strategies for maintaining their work-life balance. They had a tendency to reflect and question assumptions in the name of self-awareness and regularly took steps to adjust the things standing in their way to work-life balance.
paragraphs.zh: 卢普和鲁伊斯-卡斯特罗仔细观察了那些拒绝长时间工作的人，发现这些人在维持工作与生活平衡方面，实际上采用了惊人相似的策略。他们倾向于以自我觉察为名义去反思和质疑固有假设，并定期采取措施调整那些阻碍他们实现平衡的障碍。

paragraphs.en: Lupu and Ruiz-Castro identified five steps that the respondents in the study who had better work-life balance used in their jobs. First, they paused and reconsidered beliefs such as "I'm a professional, so I should work, work, work", and asked themselves questions like, "What's currently causing me stress?" Second, after identifying the cause, they zeroed in on their resultant emotions. Did they feel angry, sad, energised? Third, they reprioritised, asking "Is working long hours really worth cutting back on family time?", for example. Fourth, they considered their alternatives: is there anything at work that could be changed to accommodate these new priorities? And finally, they implemented changes, like asking their supervisor for greater flexibility, or deciding not to take on every project that comes their way.
paragraphs.zh: 卢普和鲁伊斯-卡斯特罗总结出研究中工作与生活平衡较好的受访者在工作中采用的五个步骤。首先，他们停下来重新审视诸如"我是专业人士，所以我应该拼命工作"之类的信条，并问自己"当前是什么在给我造成压力？"第二，在确定原因后，他们聚焦于由此产生的情绪——自己是否感到愤怒、悲伤或充满干劲？第三，他们重新调整优先级，例如自问"长时间工作真的值得牺牲家庭时间吗？"第四，他们考虑替代方案：工作中是否有任何可以改变的地方来适应这些新的优先事项？最后，他们将改变付诸实施，比如请求主管给予更大的灵活性，或是决定不再接下每一个派到自己头上的项目。

words.meaning: phr. 集中注意力于；瞄准

paragraphs.en: This five-step process is something anyone can adopt. Going through the steps, and constantly checking in with yourself, can help you shift and adapt your professional life to something that will better harmonise with your personal one. "Awareness of your emotional state is essential in order to determine the changes you want to make in your work and in your life," says Lupu.
paragraphs.zh: 这个五步流程人人都可采用。一步步践行，并时常自我审视，能帮助你调整职业生涯，使之与个人生活更加和谐共处。"要想确定自己在工作和生活中想做出哪些改变，觉察自己的情绪状态至关重要，"卢普说道。

paragraphs.en: New York University's Lechner agrees that finding that balance is an ongoing pursuit. It's not simply about dividing up the hours in your day between work, the gym, kids and chores. If the underlying emotional sources of stress are still there, then the time you actually spend at home may not be enjoyable. "We come home and even though physically we are there, mentally we still may be processing things that happened at work. We're not present," she says. What we call "work-life balance" is actually just a substitute to having a sense of fulfillment and contentment.
paragraphs.zh: 纽约大学的莱希纳也认同，找到这种平衡是一项持续的追求。这并非简单地把一天的时间分配给工作、健身、孩子和家务。如果潜在的压力情绪源头依然存在，那么你真正在家的时光可能也并不愉快。"我们回到家，尽管人已经在那儿了，但脑子里可能还在处理工作上的事。我们并没有真正在场，"她说。我们所谓的"工作与生活的平衡"，实际上只是成就感和满足感的一种替代品。

paragraphs.en: Of course, finding that balance probably shouldn't be something you have to do by yourself. Research by Erin Kelly, professor of work and organisation studies at MIT, shows companies and managers can play a key role in creating a better environment for workers. For her book Overload: How Good Jobs Went Bad and What to Do about It, she and co-author Phyllis Moen split more than 1,000 employees at a Fortune 500 company into two groups, one that worked under a management redesign and one that continued working within the existing management structure.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，寻求这种平衡或许不该是你独自承担的任务。麻省理工学院工作与组织研究教授艾琳·凯利的研究表明，公司和管理者在为员工创造更佳环境方面可以发挥关键作用。为了撰写著作《超载：好工作如何变坏以及如何应对》，她和合著者菲利斯·莫恩将一家财富500强企业的1000多名员工分成两组，一组在管理方式经过重新设计的条件下工作，另一组则继续沿用原有的管理架构。

paragraphs.en: Under the management redesign, many steps were taken to ensure better work-life balance and prevent burnout. Managers were regularly reminded to explicitly support their employees. Workers were allowed to make changes, like cancelling 9 am meetings. All of this was done in the name of increasing job satisfaction and giving workers greater flexibility, and to assure workers that it was something management was committed to. Unsurprisingly, Kelly and Moen found that employees in the redesign group reported less stress and less burnout. They were less likely to quit their jobs; indeed, over the next four years, they were 40% less likely to quit than those who kept working under the old policies.
paragraphs.zh: 在管理重新设计模式下，公司采取了诸多措施来确保更好的工作与生活平衡，防止员工过度疲劳。管理者会定期收到提醒，要求他们明确支持自己的员工。员工被允许做出调整，比如取消上午9点的会议。所有这一切，都是为了提升工作满意度，给予员工更大的灵活性，并向他们保证这是管理层决心贯彻的做法。不出所料，凯利和莫恩发现，重新设计组的员工报告的压力和倦怠感都更少。他们辞职的可能性也更低；事实上，在随后四年里，他们的辞职率比那些继续沿用旧政策的员工低了40%。

words.meaning: adj. 尽心尽力的；坚定的

paragraphs.en: "Work-life balance is understood to be an individual's response, so people think 'it's up to me to manage the craziness of my work life'", says Kelly. But organisations need to examine the demands they're placing on employees. "The root problem is not how the two pieces of work and life come together. It's that we have unrealistic expectations of what we're asked to do on the work side." If your workplace isn't an environment where work-life balance is possible in the first place, any effort you attempt to make toward it on a personal level will be in vain.
paragraphs.zh: "人们认为工作与生活的平衡要靠个人应对，所以大家都觉得'处理好我那疯狂的工作生活是我自己的事'，"凯利说。但组织需要审视自身对员工提出的要求。"根本问题不在于工作和生活这两部分如何拼凑在一起，而在于我们在工作方面被要求完成的任务本身就有着不切实际的期望。"如果你的工作场所压根儿就不是一个有可能实现工作与生活平衡的环境，那么你在个人层面为达成这种平衡所做的任何努力都将是徒劳的。

paragraphs.en: That's a conversation that appears to be gathering pace. The new prevalence of remote and flexible working models will likely all play important roles in how we balance our professional and personal lives. And if it seems like finding that perfect balance remains difficult to achieve, the experts say that keeping some perspective can help. For millions of people, work is about being able to put food on the table. Talking about work-life balance "is a very privileged conversation", says Lechner. "If we're reflecting, maybe we should also reflect on that."
paragraphs.zh: 关于这一点的讨论似乎正在加速。远程和灵活工作模式的日益普及，可能会在我们如何平衡职业与个人生活方面发挥重要作用。倘若寻找完美平衡依然看似遥不可及，专家们表示，保持一定的全局视角会有所帮助。对数以百万计的人来说，工作仅仅意味着能养家糊口。谈论工作与生活的平衡"是一场相当奢侈的对话"，莱希纳说。"如果我们要反思，或许也应该反思一下这一点。"
